noDataError: No data available for this period (no actual prices or forecast results). Please try different dates or region.
noManualDataError: No data available for this period. Please load market data before running manual simulation.

optimal.descRealized: Theoretical maximum revenue computed ex-post using actual clearing prices
optimal.descReference: {{name}} schedule revenue from its own prediction (used as 100% baseline)
optimal.tooltipRealized:
Calculation:
Optimal schedule × actual clearing prices

With known actual prices, LP solves the theoretical maximum — an upper bound that cannot be exceeded in practice.
optimal.tooltipReference:
Calculation:
Use "{{name}}" prediction schedule as baseline, calculate revenue at its own predicted price, as the comparison benchmark (= 100%).

Note: This mode is activated when actual price data is unavailable.

bestModel.descRealized: Highest revenue model when each model's schedule is settled at actual prices
bestModel.descEstimated: Highest estimated revenue among models' schedules valued at reference model's predicted price
bestModel.descReference: Highest revenue competing model (excluding baseline) settled at {{name}} predicted price
bestModel.tooltipRealized:
Calculation:
Each model's schedule → settle at actual prices → pick highest

Primary value is actual revenue; breakdown shows the model's own forecast revenue for comparison.
bestModel.tooltipEstimated:
Calculation:
Each model's own schedule → estimate with "{{priceBasis}}" predicted price → pick highest

Primary value is estimated revenue; breakdown shows result settled at actual prices under the same schedule.
bestModel.tooltipReference:
Calculation:
Each competing model × {{name}} schedule → value at {{name}} predicted price → pick highest

Baseline model itself is excluded from this mode.

efficiency.label: Strategy Efficiency
efficiency.descNormal: Best model actual revenue ÷ optimal revenue × 100%
efficiency.tooltipNormal:
Calculation:
Best model revenue ÷ optimal revenue × 100%

"Optimal" is the theoretical upper bound from actual prices.

Breakdown:
• Efficiency at actual prices: best model actual ÷ optimal
• Estimated efficiency: best model forecast ÷ optimal
  (numerator and denominator use different price bases, so often < 100%)
efficiency.tooltipReference:
Calculation:
Best competing model revenue ÷ {{name}} baseline revenue × 100%

100% means a competing model achieved the same revenue as the baseline.

manual.label: Manual Schedule
manual.desc: Expected revenue after executing the manual schedule
manual.tooltipActualLine:
• Settled at actual prices
  Manual schedule settled at actual clearing prices (most accurate result)
manual.tooltipEstimatedLine:
• Estimated at reference price
  Same schedule estimated at "{{priceBasis}}" predicted prices
manual.tooltipNote: Note: If not constrained by Cycle_limit, revenue may exceed the model optimal — for reference only.
manual.cycleLimitWarning: ⚠ Not constrained by Cycle_limit ({{limit}}), comparison for reference only

scenario.refNote: ※ Schedule reference determines suggested charge/discharge slots; revenue calculation basis is the same as KPI above.
scenario.noActualWarning: ⚠ No actual price data for this date. Applying with "Actual" as reference will set all slots to Idle. Consider selecting a model prediction as reference.
scenario.historyFetchError: Failed to load historical prices; scenario suggestions may be inaccurate.
scenario.histGapNote: (missing days filled with current day or adjacent data)
scenario.noHistoryWarning: No historical price data; will use current day prices if available.
scenario.noPriceWarning: Current day prices not yet loaded; all slots will be Idle after applying.
scenario.resetTodayTooltip: Clear all schedules for today, reset to all Idle
scenario.applyAllTooltip: Compute optimal schedule for each day independently using current scenario settings and apply in batch

descriptions.ndayAvg: References the average price at the same time slot over the past N days, setting the cheapest slots to charge and most expensive to discharge. Suitable for days with stable, low-volatility price trends. Charge/discharge slot counts are limited by battery capacity and configured cycle count.
descriptions.percentile: Uses daily price percentiles as thresholds — slots below the lower percentile charge, slots above the upper percentile discharge. Leverages the relative highs and lows of the current day's market without relying on historical data.
descriptions.fixedWindow: A time-based fixed schedule that ignores market prices, forcing charge/discharge within specified time windows. Suitable for sites with fixed operation contracts or equipment scheduling requirements.
descriptions.cycleTarget: Specify a daily target cycle count (1–3). The system automatically calculates the required slot count from battery capacity and selects the cheapest (charge) and most expensive (discharge) slots from the day's prices.
descriptions.spreadThreshold: Only executes when the average charge/discharge price spread exceeds the configured threshold; otherwise stays idle all day. Avoids low-profit or loss trades on flat-price days — suitable for conservative operations prioritizing per-trade profitability.
descriptions.peakValley: Follows Japan's common 'midnight valley, morning/evening peak' pattern: charges during early morning low-demand hours (0:00–7:30), discharges at the most expensive slots in both the morning peak (~7:30–11:30) and evening peak (~16:30–21:30).
descriptions.priceMomentum: Calculates historical price distribution for each slot over the past N days. Charges when today's price is below the historical lower percentile, discharges when above the upper percentile. Only operates when prices are relatively low or high vs. history — suitable for markets with mean-reverting price behavior.
descriptions.conservative: Discharges only in the top X% highest-price slots and charges only in the lowest X% slots, forcing large spread operations. Maximizes per-cycle revenue but with fewer trading opportunities — suitable for high battery degradation cost scenarios.

params.ndaysTooltip: How many days back to reference for average price calculation. More days = more stable but more lagged.
params.chargeSlotCountTooltip: Each slot is 30 min. Battery capacity limits the maximum (current max: {{max}} slots).
params.dischargeSlotCountTooltip: Each slot is 30 min. Battery capacity limits the maximum (current max: {{max}} slots).
params.cycleCountTooltip: Target daily charge/discharge cycles, determines slot count limit.
params.cycleCountPhysicsTooltip: Determines slot count limit (each cycle needs ~{{ch}} charge slots and {{dis}} discharge slots).
params.cycleCountTargetTooltip: Target number of full charge/discharge cycles per day; system auto-calculates required slot count.
params.cycleCountSpreadTooltip: Target number of charge/discharge cycles when spread is sufficient.
params.cycleCountMomentumTooltip: Maximum number of charge/discharge cycles to execute.
params.chargeThresholdTooltip: Only charge when daily price is below this percentile. Lower = fewer but cheaper slots.
params.dischargeThresholdTooltip: Only discharge when daily price is above this percentile. Higher = fewer but more expensive slots.
params.chargeStartTooltip: Start time for the charge window.
params.chargeEndTooltip: End time for the charge window (inclusive).
params.dischargeStartTooltip: Start time for the discharge window.
params.dischargeEndTooltip: End time for the discharge window (inclusive).
params.minSpreadTooltip: Charge/discharge average price spread must exceed this threshold; otherwise stays idle all day. Unit: ¥/kWh
params.morningPeakSlotsTooltip: Number of highest-price slots to discharge during morning peak (~7:30–11:30).
params.eveningPeakSlotsTooltip: Number of highest-price slots to discharge during evening peak (~16:30–21:30).
params.refDaysTooltip: Days used to compute historical distribution. More days = more stable baseline.
params.chargePctTooltip: Charge when today's price is below this historical percentile (e.g. 25% = cheaper than 75% of history).
params.dischargePctTooltip: Discharge when today's price is above this historical percentile (e.g. 75% = more expensive than 75% of history).
params.extremePctTooltip: Use only the top/bottom X% of prices. Lower % = fewer slots but larger spread.
params.autoCalcSlots: Charge {{ch}} slots · Discharge {{dis}} slots (auto-calculated from battery capacity)

emptyState.title: Site Revenue Simulation
emptyState.step1Desc: Select the Japanese power region and time range to analyze in the sidebar.
emptyState.step2Desc: Select one or more AI prediction models for revenue comparison.
emptyState.step3Desc: Set battery capacity, max charge/discharge power, and efficiency parameters.
emptyState.step4Desc: After data loads, the system automatically computes optimal schedules and revenue — no manual trigger required.
emptyState.instruction: Select area and model on the left; calculation starts automatically after data loads.

paramPanel.systemParamsHint: This algorithm optimizes per day; Optimal and each forecast model are computed daily and merged.
paramPanel.cycleLimit: Max equivalent full cycles over the period (total discharge ≤ value × capacity). Higher allows more cycling.

manualSidebar.title: Manual Schedule
manualSidebar.realtimeHint: Chart updates instantly after schedule changes (including SoC limits and revenue calculation).

container.simulationParamsNote: Based on your parameters, this may take a few seconds.

summary.basisDescActual: Calculate each model's backtested revenue at actual prices
summary.basisDescModel: Using "{{model}}" predicted prices to evaluate other models
summary.analysisPlaceholder: Daily revenue analysis shown after simulation

table.footnoteEstimated: Estimated Revenue uses predicted prices. Power is in kW.
table.footnoteRealized: Realized Revenue uses actual prices. Power is in kW.
